choice: Auth.js credentials with closed membership and no roles
rationale: Two binding decisions together determine this. (1) 1306ca6a establishes one household per deployment, self-deployable — there is no multi-tenant user registry, so no role model is needed, and relying on an external OAuth provider (Google) would complicate self-deployment for others. (2) bfe61279 places brokerage data behind a login on the public internet, ruling out no-auth and implying real credential-based sessions. Additionally, 5d2f9381 describes per-session E*TRADE token lifecycle (sync-on-open, one-tap reconnect) which requires individually-identified sessions, ruling out a shared passcode that provides no per-user session. Auth.js credentials with closed membership (no open registration) and no roles is the only option consistent with all three constraints: login-protected, self-deployable without external dependencies, and per-user sessions.
